title: cogswellia laevigata
intro: Cogswellia laevigata, also known as the African boxwood or boxbush, is a plant species in the family Celastraceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Cogswellia laevigata include relief from fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Cogswellia laevigata are typically made from the plant's leaves and bark, which can be brewed into teas or infused into oils for topical application.
